wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the EduFlow project overview section focused on the Arabic educational management system. Explain its purpose as a comprehensive school management platform built with Python and Flask, its target audience of Arabic-speaking educational institutions, and key capabilities including student management, grade tracking, academic year management, and multi-role dashboards. Document the system's architecture, technology stack overview, and relationship to educational workflows. Include both conceptual overviews for stakeholders and technical highlights for developers. Use terminology consistent with the codebase such as 'school admin', 'teacher portal', and 'student portal'. Provide practical examples demonstrating the system's impact on educational institution operations. Document the multi-school support architecture and Arabic RTL interface implementation.

wiki_catalogs.name: System Introduction
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the EduFlow system introduction section focused on the Arabic educational management platform. Explain the system's purpose as a comprehensive school management solution designed for Arabic-speaking educational institutions. Document the core mission of streamlining administrative tasks, academic tracking, and multi-role access control. Describe the target audience including schools, educational administrators, teachers, and students in Arabic-speaking regions. Include the system's value proposition in terms of digital transformation for educational institutions. Provide context about the educational challenges the system addresses and how it improves operational efficiency. Use terminology consistent with the codebase and educational domain. Include practical examples of daily workflows that the system enables.

wiki_catalogs.name: JWT Token Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the JWT token management system in EduFlow. Detail the TokenManager class implementation including access token and refresh token generation with HS256 algorithm. Document token payload structure containing user data, timestamps, and unique identifiers. Explain the token lifecycle from generation through verification to refresh and blacklisting. Include practical examples of token creation, validation, and renewal processes. Document the token blacklist mechanism for logout and revocation. Address token security features including unique token IDs (JTI), expiration handling, and token hashing for blacklisting. Provide code examples showing proper token usage patterns and error handling scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: Schema Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive schema overview documentation for the EduFlow database design. Detail the complete entity relationship model including all 11 tables: users, schools, students, teachers, subjects, grade_levels, teacher_subjects, teacher_class_assignments, system_academic_years, academic_years, student_grades, and student_attendance. Document the primary key and foreign key relationships between entities, explaining how schools isolate data through foreign key constraints, how students are linked to schools, and how teachers are connected to subjects and classes. Include table schemas with field definitions, data types, constraints, and indexes. Explain the dual MySQL/SQLite support architecture and how the database abstraction layer maintains consistency across both database engines. Document the many-to-many relationships using junction tables like teacher_subjects and teacher_class_assignments.

wiki_catalogs.name: System Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the EduFlow system architecture. Explain the layered architecture pattern with Flask web framework as the foundation, including the separation between presentation layer (HTML/CSS/JavaScript), API layer (Flask routes), business logic layer (services), and data access layer (database abstraction). Document the component relationships showing how frontend HTML pages interact with API endpoints, which delegate to business services, and how data flows through the system. Include architectural diagrams illustrating the request-response flow, component dependencies, and data persistence patterns. Address the technical decisions behind choosing Flask for API development, the layered approach for maintainability, and the modular design that supports future extensions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication Endpoints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for authentication endpoints. Document the three main authentication routes: /api/admin/login (administrator authentication), /api/school/login (school administrator login), and /api/student/login (student portal access). For each endpoint, specify HTTP method (POST), request body parameters (username/password for admin, school code for school login, student code for student login), response format including success flag, token generation with JWT, and user/school/student data. Include authentication requirements, error handling patterns with Arabic error messages, rate limiting exemptions, and security considerations. Provide practical examples of successful authentication requests and responses, token structure, and integration guidelines for client applications.

wiki_catalogs.name: Student Management System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the student management system within EduFlow. Detail the complete student enrollment process including student registration, unique student code generation, and profile management. Document the academic record tracking system with detailed score management, grade level validation, and score scaling for elementary vs secondary education. Explain the student profile structure including personal information, medical details, parent contact information, and emergency data. Cover the grade level system with Arabic educational stages (elementary, middle, secondary, preparatory) and their corresponding grade mappings. Document the student deletion and modification workflows, data validation rules, and duplicate prevention mechanisms. Include practical examples of student enrollment scenarios, score entry workflows, and profile updates. Address the integration with school management and teacher assignment systems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Arabic RTL Interface
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Arabic RTL (Right-to-Left) interface implementation in EduFlow. Explain the Arabic language support with proper RTL layout direction, font rendering using Cairo font family, and bidirectional text handling. Document the RTL CSS architecture including flexbox and grid layouts adapted for Arabic text direction, icon positioning, and form element alignment. Cover accessibility features for Arabic users including screen reader support, keyboard navigation patterns, and ARIA attributes. Detail the responsive design system that works across Arabic RTL contexts, mobile-first approach for Arabic-speaking regions, and cross-browser compatibility for RTL interfaces. Include practical examples of Arabic text rendering, RTL component positioning, and cultural localization aspects specific to Arabic educational environments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Score Management System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the score management system component. Document the dual-scale grading implementation with 10-point scale for elementary grades (1-4) and 100-point scale for higher grades. Explain the score validation rules, grade conversion algorithms, and score entry workflows. Detail the subject-specific scoring mechanisms, score modification procedures, and score aggregation calculations. Include practical examples of score entry scenarios, grade book management, and score validation processes. Address the integration with student profiles, subject assignments, and academic year tracking. Document the score persistence mechanisms and data integrity checks.

wiki_catalogs.name: Teacher Assignment System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the teacher assignment system that manages classroom assignments and subject specializations. Document the assign_teacher_to_class and remove_teacher_from_class functions, including parameter validation, conflict detection, and assignment persistence. Explain the teacher class assignment workflow from initial assignment through removal, including database transactions and constraint enforcement. Detail the get_teacher_class_assignments function for retrieving teacher schedules and the get_class_teachers function for class roster management. Cover the teacher-student relationship mapping for class management and grade supervision. Include examples of teacher assignment scenarios, classroom management workflows, and assignment conflict resolution. Address the integration with subject management and grade tracking systems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Student Enrollment Process
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the student enrollment process within EduFlow. Document the complete enrollment workflow including student registration form processing, unique student code generation (STD- prefix with timestamp and random suffix), duplicate prevention mechanisms, and validation rules. Explain the enrollment data structure with required fields (full_name, grade, room, enrollment_date) and optional medical information (parent_contact, blood_type, chronic_disease). Cover the grade validation system including educational level checking (ابتدائي, متوسطة, ثانوية, إعدادية) and grade format validation. Detail the duplicate detection algorithm that prevents multiple enrollments of the same student name in the same grade. Include practical examples of successful enrollment scenarios, error handling for invalid data, and integration with the school management system. Address the relationship between student enrollment and classroom assignment workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Performance Dashboards
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the performance dashboard system. Explain the real-time performance monitoring dashboard that displays student achievement tracking, class performance metrics, and academic progress indicators. Document the dashboard widgets including grade distribution charts, attendance analytics, and performance trend visualizations. Detail the data aggregation mechanisms for real-time metrics collection and display. Cover the administrative dashboard features for school management oversight, teacher evaluation metrics, and academic program assessment. Include examples of dashboard configuration, widget customization, and performance metric interpretation. Address the integration with the performance monitoring system and data refresh mechanisms.

wiki_catalogs.name: Administrator Portal
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Administrator Portal in EduFlow. Detail the system-wide administrative interface including school management capabilities, academic year administration, and system configuration options. Document the portal's main components: school creation and management forms, academic year setup and monitoring, school listing with filtering capabilities, and export functionality. Explain the administrative workflow for adding new schools with automatic code generation, managing study types and educational stages, and handling gender-specific school classifications. Cover the academic year management system including manual creation, automatic generation, and current year designation. Include examples of administrative actions, form validation patterns, modal dialog implementations, and data export processes. Address the portal's security model, role-based permissions, and integration with backend administrative APIs.

wiki_catalogs.name: Teacher-Class Assignment Workflow
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the teacher-class assignment workflow that handles classroom assignments and teacher-student relationships. Document the assign_teacher_to_class function implementation including parameter validation, conflict detection, and assignment persistence. Explain the complete workflow from initial teacher assignment through classroom enrollment, including database transactions and constraint enforcement. Detail the assignment validation process that prevents duplicate assignments and ensures proper teacher-student relationships. Include examples of successful assignment scenarios, parameter requirements, and error handling for invalid assignments. Address the integration with student enrollment and grade tracking systems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Grade Entry Permissions System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the grade entry permissions system that controls teacher access to student grade modifications. Document the permission matrix that determines which teachers can modify grades for specific subjects and students. Explain the grade scale conversion logic for elementary (ابتدائي) grades 1-4 using 10-point scale versus 100-point scale for higher grades. Detail the teacher-student relationship mapping that validates which educators have authorization to update academic records. Include examples of permission workflows, grade modification scenarios, and the integration with subject-specialization requirements. Address grade validation rules, scale conversions, and audit trail requirements for grade changes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Score Scaling Algorithms
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the score scaling algorithms used in academic record management. Document the grade-level score validation system that automatically adjusts score ranges based on educational stage (grades 1-4 use 0-10 scale, others use 0-100 scale). Explain the mathematical formulas and conversion algorithms used to maintain consistency across different grading scales. Detail the score normalization processes that ensure fair comparison between subjects and assessment periods. Cover the validation logic that prevents invalid score entries and maintains data integrity across the entire academic record system. Include practical examples of score scaling scenarios, edge case handling, and integration with grade calculation workflows. Address the impact of score scaling on final grade computation and report generation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Attendance Tracking System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the attendance tracking system component. Document the attendance recording mechanisms, daily attendance registers, and automated absence tracking. Explain the integration with class schedules and teacher-student relationships. Detail the attendance data collection workflows, reporting mechanisms, and real-time attendance updates. Include examples of attendance entry processes, absence categorization, and automated notifications for excessive absences. Address the data validation rules, attendance statistics calculation, and integration with academic year progression.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for the EduFlow system. Cover prerequisites including Python environment setup, Flask framework knowledge, and database administration skills. Document step-by-step installation procedures including dependency installation with pip, environment configuration using .env.example, database setup and initialization, and server startup. Include system requirements, supported platforms, and initial configuration steps. Provide practical examples of environment variable configuration, database connection setup, and first-time system initialization. Address common installation issues and their solutions. Document the basic workflow from installation completion to accessing the admin dashboard.

wiki_catalogs.name: Key Features
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the EduFlow key features section focused on the core capabilities of the educational management system. Document the five main features including student management, grade level tracking, academic year management, admin dashboard, and school dashboard. Explain each feature's functionality, user benefits, and implementation approach. Detail the multi-role portal system with admin, school admin, teacher, and student dashboards. Include the Arabic RTL interface capabilities and responsive design features. Document the real-time data synchronization, reporting capabilities, and integration patterns. Provide concrete examples of feature usage scenarios and their impact on educational institution operations. Address the modular nature of features and how they work together as an integrated system.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication Middleware
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the authentication middleware system. Document the AuthMiddleware class and its Flask route decorators including authenticate_request and optional_auth. Explain the authentication flow from Authorization header extraction through token verification to role-based access control. Detail the decorator pattern implementation for securing API endpoints. Include practical examples of applying authentication decorators to different routes with various role requirements. Document the request context integration storing current user information and access tokens. Address error handling for missing tokens, invalid tokens, and insufficient permissions. Provide examples of both mandatory and optional authentication scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: Table Definitions
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed table definition documentation for each database table in the EduFlow system. For each table, document field names, data types, constraints (NOT NULL, UNIQUE, PRIMARY KEY), default values, and foreign key relationships. Include detailed descriptions of specialized fields like JSON columns (detailed_scores, daily_attendance), boolean flags (is_current), and computed fields. Document the purpose and business logic behind each field, explaining how they support the educational management functionality. Cover table creation scripts, column addition for migrations, and data validation rules. Include examples of typical data stored in each table and explain the significance of unique constraints like school codes and teacher codes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Flow Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the data flow architecture in EduFlow. Document the complete data pathway from frontend requests through API endpoints to database operations and caching layers. Explain the request processing flow including input sanitization, authentication, business logic execution, database queries, and response generation. Detail the caching strategy using Redis for performance optimization, including cache invalidation, data consistency, and cache warming. Document the performance monitoring integration that tracks request latency, error rates, and system health. Include sequence diagrams showing typical user workflows like login, student enrollment, and grade updates. Address data validation, transformation, and error handling throughout the pipeline.

wiki_catalogs.name: School Management API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for school management endpoints. Document GET /api/schools for retrieving all schools with sorting and filtering capabilities, POST /api/schools for adding new schools with validation for name, study_type, level, and gender_type fields, PUT /api/schools/<school_id> for updating existing schools, and DELETE /api/schools/<school_id> for removing schools. Include request/response schemas, field validation rules, Arabic error messages, role-based access control (admin-only), and data transformation logic. Provide examples of school creation with automatic code generation, update operations with timestamp tracking, and deletion scenarios with proper error handling.

wiki_catalogs.name: Academic Year Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the academic year management system. Explain the academic year lifecycle including creation, activation, and progression logic. Document the year generation algorithms for upcoming academic years and automatic year advancement. Detail the grade level progression system with promotion criteria, grade advancement rules, and academic year validation. Cover the relationship between academic years and student enrollment, including year-specific student records and grade level tracking. Explain the academic year configuration options, start/end date management, and year-specific settings. Include examples of academic year setup, student year progression workflows, and multi-year academic tracking. Address the integration with student enrollment, teacher assignments, and subject management systems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Role-Specific Portals
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the role-specific portal architecture in EduFlow. Document the four distinct user portals: admin dashboard for system-wide management, school dashboard for institutional operations, teacher portal for classroom management, and student portal for academic tracking. Explain the portal navigation patterns, role-based content filtering, and permission-driven feature access. Detail the portal-specific UI components, data visualization elements, and interactive features tailored to each user role. Cover the portal routing mechanisms, session management per role, and data isolation between different portal types. Include examples of role-specific workflows, portal customization options, and the relationship between portal interfaces and backend API endpoints. Address portal security considerations, role switching capabilities, and user experience optimization for each portal type.

wiki_catalogs.name: Attendance Tracking System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the attendance tracking system. Detail the daily attendance recording mechanisms, absence management procedures, and attendance analytics capabilities. Explain the attendance status definitions, late arrival tracking, and early departure recording. Document the attendance report generation, monthly summaries, and yearly attendance statistics. Include practical examples of attendance entry workflows, bulk attendance updates, and attendance exception handling. Address the integration with student schedules, class management, and academic reporting systems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Student-Teacher Interaction Workflows
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for student-teacher interaction workflows including grade entry permissions, student progress monitoring, and academic advising. Detail the teacher-student relationship mapping for class management and supervision responsibilities. Document the grade entry permissions system that controls which teachers can modify student grades and when. Explain the student progress monitoring workflows including attendance tracking, behavioral monitoring, and academic performance evaluation. Cover the academic advising system for teacher-student consultation and guidance. Include examples of typical interaction scenarios, permission workflows, and progress reporting mechanisms. Address the integration with grading systems, attendance tracking, and performance analytics.

wiki_catalogs.name: Academic Record Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the academic record management system. Detail the detailed scoring system including subject-based grade tracking, period-based assessment recording, and score validation mechanisms. Document the grade scale system with elementary grades 1-4 using 10-point scale versus standard 100-point scale for other grades. Explain the score validation logic that automatically checks score ranges based on grade level and throws appropriate errors for invalid entries. Cover the daily attendance tracking system with JSON-based data structures for storing attendance records. Detail the score scaling algorithms for different educational stages and how they affect grade calculations. Include practical examples of score entry workflows, bulk score updates, and grade level validation scenarios. Address the integration with teacher assignment systems and how academic records feed into performance analytics.

wiki_catalogs.name: Report Generation System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the report generation system. Explain the academic transcript generation workflow, progress report creation, and performance summary compilation. Document the report templates, formatting options, and data extraction processes. Detail the integration with student records, grade calculations, and academic history processing. Cover the report scheduling system, bulk generation capabilities, and export formats. Include examples of report generation APIs, template customization, and automated report delivery. Address the report validation processes, quality checks, and error handling mechanisms.

wiki_catalogs.name: School Dashboard Portal
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the School Dashboard Portal, the central operational interface for school administrators. Document the portal's core functionality including student enrollment management, teacher assignment coordination, academic year tracking, and institutional reporting. Explain the dashboard layout with academic year display, school information panels, and operational controls. Detail the student management features including enrollment forms, class assignments, and academic record tracking. Cover the teacher management components for staff allocation, subject assignments, and workload distribution. Document the portal's navigation patterns, data filtering capabilities, and report generation features. Include examples of school administrative workflows, data entry procedures, and integration with the broader educational management system. Address the portal's role in coordinating between different educational levels and managing institutional resources.

wiki_catalogs.name: Teacher-Class Scheduling Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for teacher-class scheduling management that retrieves and manages teacher schedules and class rosters. Document the get_teacher_class_assignments function for retrieving teacher schedules including subject assignments, grade levels, and classroom information. Explain the get_class_teachers function for class roster management and teacher availability checking. Detail the scheduling algorithms that handle teacher workload distribution and classroom capacity management. Include examples of schedule retrieval scenarios, teacher availability queries, and class roster operations. Address the integration with subject management and grade level tracking systems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Student Progress Monitoring Workflows
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for student progress monitoring workflows including attendance tracking, behavioral monitoring, and academic performance evaluation. Detail the teacher-student supervision responsibilities and class management integration. Document the progress reporting mechanisms that aggregate grade data, attendance records, and behavioral indicators. Explain the automated progress alerts and notification systems for teachers and administrators. Include examples of typical monitoring scenarios, data aggregation patterns, and performance analytics dashboards. Address the integration with grading systems, attendance tracking, and real-time progress updates.

wiki_catalogs.name: Grade Calculation Engine
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the grade calculation engine that processes academic records. Detail the algorithms used to compute final grades from individual subject scores and assessment period results. Explain the weighted average calculations, grade point averaging, and cumulative grade computation methods. Document the grade level progression logic that determines student advancement based on academic performance. Cover the integration with score validation systems and how invalid scores are handled during calculation processes. Include practical examples of grade calculation scenarios, bulk grade updates, and grade verification workflows. Address the relationship between grade calculation and academic promotion systems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Academic Evaluation Workflows
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for academic evaluation workflows including grade management, assessment tracking, and performance analysis. Detail the grade entry systems, grading scale conversions (10-point vs 100-point scales), and academic progress calculations. Document the integration with subject assignments, teacher-student relationships, and class management. Explain the automated grade aggregation, performance trend analysis, and report generation systems. Include examples of typical evaluation scenarios, grade distribution analysis, and academic warning systems for underperforming students.

wiki_catalogs.name: Architecture Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the EduFlow system. Describe the high-level layered architecture with Flask web framework, database abstraction layer, authentication service, and security middleware. Document component interactions between frontend HTML pages, API endpoints, business logic services, and database layer. Explain technical decisions including the choice of Flask for API development, dual MySQL/SQLite support, and JWT-based authentication. Include system boundaries, data flow architecture, and integration patterns. Address cross-cutting concerns like caching with Redis, performance monitoring, and security middleware. Document technology stack including Flask 3.0.0, PyJWT 2.8.0, bcrypt 4.0.1, and mysql-connector-python 8.0.33. Provide system context diagrams showing how different components interact and data flows through the system.

wiki_catalogs.name: Technology Stack
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the EduFlow technology stack section focused on the technical foundation of the educational management system. Document the core technologies including Python 3.x, Flask web framework, MySQL database, and Redis caching. Explain the choice of technologies and their roles in the system architecture. Detail the frontend technologies including HTML5, CSS3, JavaScript, and Arabic RTL support implementation. Document the security technologies including JWT authentication, bcrypt encryption, and input sanitization. Include the hosting technologies and deployment configurations for Render, Railway, and Vercel platforms. Address the development tools, testing frameworks, and quality assurance technologies. Provide version compatibility information and upgrade paths. Explain the rationale behind technology choices for educational institution requirements.

wiki_catalogs.name: Role-Based Access Control
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the role-based access control system. Document the multi-role architecture supporting admin, school admin, teacher, and student roles. Explain role validation during authentication and authorization checks. Detail the role hierarchy and permission matrix for different portal types. Include practical examples of role-based endpoint protection and conditional access patterns. Document the integration between authentication tokens and role validation. Address role transitions and permission inheritance patterns. Provide examples of implementing role-specific business logic and data access restrictions. Document the relationship between user roles and portal access levels.

wiki_catalogs.name: Relationships & Constraints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for database relationships and constraints in the EduFlow system. Explain the foreign key relationships between tables, including cascading deletes from schools to students, and cascade updates from system_academic_years to student records. Document the many-to-many relationships using junction tables (teacher_subjects, teacher_class_assignments) and explain the business logic behind these relationships. Detail unique constraints, composite unique keys (like teacher-class-subject-academic_year combinations), and check constraints. Explain the tenant isolation mechanism using school_id foreign keys and how it prevents data leakage between schools. Document referential integrity rules, constraint names, and the impact of constraint violations on data operations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Design Patterns
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the design patterns implemented in EduFlow. Document the Repository Pattern used in database abstraction for testability and separation of concerns. Explain the Factory Pattern implementation for dynamic service creation based on role requirements and feature needs. Detail the Observer Pattern usage for real-time data synchronization between clients and server. Document the Middleware Pattern implementation in security middleware for cross-cutting concerns like authentication, rate limiting, and input sanitization. Include the Decorator Pattern usage for authentication and authorization decorators. Provide code examples from the actual implementation showing how each pattern is applied, the benefits they provide, and how they contribute to system maintainability and extensibility.

wiki_catalogs.name: Student Management API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for student management endpoints. Document GET /api/school/<school_id>/students for retrieving all students in a school with detailed academic data, POST /api/school/<school_id>/student for adding new students with validation for full_name, grade, room, and optional medical/demographic fields, PUT /api/student/<student_id> for updating student information, and DELETE /api/student/<student_id> for removing students. Include detailed validation rules for grade format, blood type validation, duplicate detection, and academic data handling. Document the elementary vs secondary grade scoring systems (0-10 vs 0-100 scales), detailed_scores and daily_attendance JSON fields, and comprehensive error handling with Arabic localization.

wiki_catalogs.name: Grading and Assessment System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the grading and assessment system. Detail the dual-scale grading system supporting both 10-point scale for elementary grades (1-4) and 100-point scale for higher grades. Document the detailed score management including subject-specific scoring, period-based assessments, and score validation rules. Explain the score entry workflows for teachers, score modification procedures, and score aggregation mechanisms. Cover the attendance tracking system with daily attendance recording, absence management, and attendance analytics. Detail the grade calculation algorithms, score averaging, and performance evaluation methods. Include practical examples of score entry scenarios, grade book management, and assessment reporting. Address the integration with student profiles, subject assignments, and academic year tracking.

wiki_catalogs.name: Form Validation System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the form validation system implementation in EduFlow. Document the validation architecture including client-side validation rules, real-time feedback mechanisms, and error handling patterns. Explain the distinction between mutable and immutable validation approaches, form state management, and dynamic validation updates. Detail the validation rules for different input types including Arabic text validation, numeric inputs, date formats, and special character restrictions. Cover the integration with backend validation, AJAX form submission handling, and error message localization in Arabic. Include examples of validation patterns for login forms, registration forms, and data entry forms across different portals. Address validation performance optimization, browser compatibility, and accessibility compliance for form validation feedback.

wiki_catalogs.name: Evaluation and Performance Algorithms
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the evaluation and performance calculation algorithms. Document the grade calculation methods, score averaging techniques, and performance evaluation formulas. Explain the weighted scoring systems, grade point calculations, and academic standing determinations. Detail the promotion algorithms, grade advancement criteria, and academic probation detection. Include practical examples of grade calculation scenarios, performance analytics computations, and evaluation report generation. Address the integration with grading scales, academic year progression, and student academic history tracking.

wiki_catalogs.name: Class Insights and Analytics
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the class insights system that provides teacher performance analytics and student achievement tracking. Document the get_class_insights function and related analytics queries that aggregate student performance data by class, subject, and time period. Explain the teacher performance metrics including student success rates, grade distributions, and attendance patterns. Detail the student achievement tracking system that monitors individual and collective progress over time. Cover the analytics dashboard functionality for teacher self-assessment and improvement planning. Include examples of analytics reports, performance benchmarking, and trend analysis. Address the integration with grading systems, attendance tracking, and academic year progression.

wiki_catalogs.name: Student Profile Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for student profile management within EduFlow. Detail the complete student profile structure including mandatory information (full_name, student_code, grade, room) and optional medical/demographic data (parent_contact, blood_type, chronic_disease). Document the profile update workflows including partial updates, complete profile modifications, and data validation rules. Explain the profile search and retrieval mechanisms with filtering by grade, room, and school. Cover the profile data integrity measures including duplicate prevention and data sanitization. Detail the integration with other school systems including teacher assignments, subject enrollment, and academic progress tracking. Include practical examples of profile creation, update scenarios, and search workflows. Address the relationship between student profiles and authentication systems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Visualization Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the data visualization system. Explain the chart and graph rendering capabilities including grade distribution visualizations, attendance analytics charts, and performance trend displays. Document the interactive dashboard components, drill-down capabilities, and real-time data updates. Detail the integration with the caching system for optimized visualization performance. Cover the responsive design aspects for mobile and desktop visualization display. Include examples of chart configuration, data binding, and visualization customization options. Address the performance optimization techniques and data aggregation for efficient rendering.

wiki_catalogs.name: Teacher Portal
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Teacher Portal, the specialized interface for classroom instruction and student assessment. Document the portal's key features including class management, student grade entry, subject-specific operations, and performance recommendations. Explain the enhanced teacher portal functionality including improved subject assignment interfaces, recommendation systems for student progress, and streamlined grade entry workflows. Detail the teacher's daily operational tasks including student attendance tracking, academic performance monitoring, and report generation. Cover the subject assignment system that links teachers to specific courses and grade levels. Document the portal's integration with student records, grade databases, and academic year tracking systems. Include examples of teacher workflows, grade entry procedures, student assessment patterns, and performance analytics. Address the portal's role in supporting instructional activities and maintaining academic standards.

wiki_catalogs.name: Teacher Removal and Conflict Resolution
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for teacher removal processes and conflict resolution mechanisms in the assignment system. Document the remove_teacher_from_class function implementation including parameter validation, dependency checking, and safe removal procedures. Explain conflict detection algorithms that identify scheduling conflicts, resource conflicts, and assignment violations. Detail the conflict resolution workflow that handles teacher removal scenarios, replacement assignment procedures, and student reassignment processes. Include examples of removal operations, conflict scenarios, and resolution strategies. Address the integration with student transfer systems and grade continuity maintenance.

wiki_catalogs.name: Academic Advising and Consultation System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the academic advising system that facilitates teacher-student consultation and guidance workflows. Document the advising session scheduling, progress counseling, and academic planning features. Explain the teacher-student relationship mapping for advisory responsibilities and mentorship coordination. Detail the consultation logging system that tracks advising sessions, student progress discussions, and academic recommendations. Include examples of typical advising scenarios, session workflows, and progress tracking integration. Address the integration with student academic records, performance analytics, and administrative oversight.

wiki_catalogs.name: Behavioral Monitoring System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the behavioral monitoring system including conduct tracking, disciplinary records, and behavioral intervention protocols. Document the behavioral incident reporting mechanisms, student behavior classification systems, and automated behavioral alerts. Explain the integration with teacher supervision responsibilities and class management workflows. Detail the behavioral data collection, trend analysis, and reporting systems for administrators. Include examples of behavioral monitoring scenarios, intervention strategies, and collaboration between teachers, students, and parents.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication & Security
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive authentication and security documentation for the EduFlow system. Document the multi-role authentication system supporting admin, school admin, teacher, and student roles with JWT token management. Explain the security middleware implementation including rate limiting, input sanitization with Bleach, and validation helpers. Detail the authentication flow from login requests through token generation to session management. Document security measures including bcrypt password hashing, PyOTP integration for two-factor authentication, and input validation patterns. Include practical examples of authentication endpoints, token handling, and security middleware usage. Address common security vulnerabilities and their mitigation strategies. Document the relationship between authentication and authorization systems, and how role-based access control is implemented across different portal types.

wiki_catalogs.name: Target Audience
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the EduFlow target audience section focused on identifying and understanding the users of the educational management system. Document the primary user groups including educational administrators, school principals, teachers, students, and system integrators. Explain the specific needs and workflows of each user group and how the system addresses their requirements. Detail the geographical focus on Arabic-speaking regions and cultural considerations in the system design. Include the technical proficiency requirements for different user roles and the training implications. Document the institutional context including school sizes, regional variations, and implementation considerations. Address the scalability aspects for different types of educational institutions. Provide guidance on user adoption strategies and change management approaches for educational environments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Input Validation and Sanitization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for input validation and sanitization systems. Document the validation helper functions and Bleach integration for HTML sanitization. Explain input validation patterns for different data types including strings, numbers, and special characters. Detail the sanitization process for preventing XSS attacks and data injection. Include practical examples of validation decorators and helper functions. Document the integration with form processing and API request validation. Address common validation scenarios for user registration, login forms, and data entry. Provide examples of custom validation rules and error handling patterns. Document the relationship between validation and security middleware.

wiki_catalogs.name: Migration Strategies
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive migration strategy documentation for the EduFlow database system. Detail the database initialization process, table creation with backward compatibility, and column addition strategies for schema evolution. Explain the migration from legacy academic_years table to system_academic_years table, including data preservation and transition strategies. Document the fallback mechanisms when MySQL is unavailable, automatic SQLite conversion, and environment-based database selection. Cover database versioning approaches, schema update procedures, and rollback strategies. Include examples of ALTER TABLE operations for adding new columns, data transformation during migrations, and handling of existing data during schema changes. Address performance considerations during large-scale migrations and data consistency verification.

wiki_catalogs.name: Technology Stack
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed content for the EduFlow technology stack. Document the complete technology ecosystem including Flask 3.0.0 as the web framework, PyJWT 2.8.0 for secure token management, bcrypt 4.0.1 for password hashing, and mysql-connector-python 8.0.33 for database connectivity. Explain the frontend technologies including HTML5, CSS3 with Arabic RTL support, JavaScript ES6+, and modern web standards. Detail the hosting platform configurations for Render, Railway, and Vercel, including environment variable management and deployment automation. Document the development tools including VS Code configuration, Python virtual environments, and testing frameworks. Include version compatibility matrices, dependency relationships, and upgrade considerations for maintaining the technology stack.

wiki_catalogs.name: Teacher Management API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for teacher management endpoints. Document GET /api/school/<school_id>/teachers for retrieving teachers with subject assignments and class mappings, POST /api/school/<school_id>/teacher for adding new teachers with unique teacher codes, and related endpoints for teacher-student relationships and subject assignments. Include teacher authentication flow, subject specialization management, classroom assignment operations, and teacher-student relationship mapping. Document the teacher code generation system, subject assignment validation, and integration with the teacher portal. Provide examples of teacher registration, subject assignment workflows, and class management operations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Subject Management System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the subject management system. Explain the subject creation and configuration process including subject naming, grade level associations, and curriculum alignment. Document the subject assignment system linking subjects to specific grade levels and educational stages. Detail the subject-based score tracking including subject-specific grade records, assessment categories, and performance metrics. Cover the subject deletion and modification workflows, subject duplication prevention, and grade-level validation. Explain the relationship between subjects and teacher assignments, classroom management, and student enrollment. Include examples of subject setup workflows, grade-level subject configurations, and subject-based reporting. Address the integration with teacher management, student enrollment, and academic year systems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Design System Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the unified design system implementation in EduFlow. Document the design tokens including color palette with education-appropriate colors, typography scale using Cairo font family, spacing system, and component library. Explain the CSS architecture with modular design system implementation, custom properties for theme consistency, and component-based styling patterns. Detail the component library including buttons, cards, forms, navigation elements, and layout components with their variants and states. Cover the design system's integration with RTL layout, responsive breakpoints, and accessibility compliance. Include examples of design system usage across different portals, customization options for educational institutions, and maintenance guidelines for design system evolution. Address design system performance optimization, browser compatibility, and contribution guidelines for extending the design system.

wiki_catalogs.name: Assessment Reporting System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the assessment reporting system. Detail the report generation mechanisms, progress tracking displays, and academic performance dashboards. Explain the report templates, data formatting standards, and multi-format export capabilities (PDF, Excel, CSV). Document the real-time reporting features, automated report scheduling, and stakeholder access controls. Include practical examples of report creation workflows, custom report configurations, and report distribution mechanisms. Address the integration with student profiles, teacher dashboards, and administrative reporting requirements.

wiki_catalogs.name: Grade Level and Educational Stages
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the grade level and educational stage system in EduFlow. Document the four educational stages (ابتدائي - Elementary, متوسطة - Middle, ثانوية - Secondary, إعدادية - Preparatory) and their corresponding grade mappings. Explain the grade string format including stage indicators and grade descriptions (e.g., 'ابتدائي - الأول الابتدائي'). Detail the grade validation system that ensures proper stage assignment and prevents invalid grade combinations. Cover the grade progression logic and how students move between grades within the academic year system. Document the grade-based score scaling system that automatically applies appropriate score ranges based on educational stage. Include practical examples of grade assignment scenarios, validation rules, and integration with subject assignment systems. Address the relationship between grade levels and curriculum requirements.

wiki_catalogs.name: Analytics and Metrics Collection
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the analytics and metrics collection system. Explain the educational metrics tracking including student performance indicators, class effectiveness measures, and academic program outcomes. Document the real-time monitoring system for system performance, user activity tracking, and educational metrics collection. Detail the data aggregation pipelines, statistical analysis functions, and trend detection algorithms. Cover the administrative analytics features for decision support, resource allocation insights, and performance benchmarking. Include examples of metric calculation workflows, data export capabilities, and custom analytics development. Address the integration with external analytics platforms and data privacy considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Student Portal
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the Student Portal, the academic tracking interface for learners and their families. Document the portal's core functionality including academic progress monitoring, grade viewing, attendance records, and performance reports. Explain the student's access to personal academic information, course enrollment status, and grade history. Detail the portal's user-friendly interface designed for Arabic RTL navigation, including academic year displays, subject listings, and grade visualization components. Cover the portal's integration with student records, teacher communications, and academic calendar systems. Document the student's ability to track progress across subjects, view performance trends, and access academic resources. Include examples of student workflows, grade checking procedures, attendance monitoring, and academic planning features. Address the portal's role in supporting student learning and enabling family involvement in education tracking.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Design
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive database design documentation for the EduFlow system. Detail the entity relationship model including users (admin, school, teacher, student), schools with unique codes, students with academic records, teachers with subject specializations, and academic year structures. Document table schemas, primary/foreign key relationships, indexes, and constraints. Explain the dual MySQL/SQLite support architecture and database abstraction layer implementation. Include database schema diagrams showing relationships between entities like school-user associations, student-enrollment tracking, and teacher-subject mappings. Document data validation rules, business logic constraints, and data lifecycle management. Address database migration strategies, schema updates, and data integrity preservation. Include performance considerations for educational data queries and reporting operations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Password Security
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for password security implementation. Document the bcrypt integration for secure password hashing and verification. Explain password strength requirements and validation patterns. Detail the password reset workflow and security measures. Include examples of password hashing during user registration and authentication. Document the integration with the overall authentication system. Address password policy enforcement and security best practices. Provide examples of secure password handling throughout the application lifecycle. Document the relationship between password security and session management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Performance Optimization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed performance optimization documentation for the EduFlow database system. Document indexing strategies for frequently queried columns like school_id, student_code, teacher_code, and academic_year_id. Explain query optimization techniques for complex joins involving multiple tables (teachers, subjects, assignments, students). Cover database connection pooling configuration, SQLite vs MySQL performance characteristics, and query execution plans. Document caching strategies for educational data, batch operations for bulk data processing, and optimization of report generation queries. Include performance monitoring approaches, slow query identification, and database tuning recommendations. Address scalability considerations for multi-school deployments, connection limits, and resource allocation strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Subject Management API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for subject management endpoints. Document GET /api/school/<school_id>/subjects for retrieving all subjects with grade-level sorting, POST /api/school/<school_id>/subject for adding new subjects with validation for name and grade_level fields, and related endpoints for subject-specific operations. Include subject classification by grade levels, subject-teacher assignment workflows, and integration with student grade tracking systems. Document the subject filtering mechanisms, grade-level mapping, and academic year progression logic. Provide examples of subject creation, grade-level categorization, and subject-specific data management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Teacher-Student Relationship Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for managing relationships between teachers and students. Detail the teacher assignment system including classroom assignments, subject specialization tracking, and teaching load management. Document the student-teacher relationship mapping for class management, grade supervision, and academic support. Explain the teacher recommendation system for optimal student placement and subject assignment. Cover the teacher-student interaction workflows including grade entry permissions, student progress monitoring, and academic advising. Detail the class insights system for teacher performance analytics and student achievement tracking. Include examples of teacher assignment scenarios, classroom management workflows, and student progress reporting. Address the integration with subject management, grading systems, and performance analytics.

wiki_catalogs.name: Responsive & Mobile Optimization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for responsive design and mobile optimization in EduFlow. Document the mobile-first responsive architecture including breakpoint strategy, flexible grid systems, and adaptive component layouts. Explain the touch-friendly interface design with appropriate button sizes, gesture support, and mobile navigation patterns. Detail the viewport configuration, meta tag optimizations, and mobile-specific performance considerations. Cover the responsive typography system, image optimization techniques, and mobile-specific form layouts. Include examples of responsive component behavior across different screen sizes, mobile navigation patterns, and touch interaction design. Address mobile performance optimization, progressive enhancement strategies, and cross-platform compatibility for educational tablet and smartphone usage in Arabic-speaking regions.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Reference
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for the EduFlow RESTful API endpoints. Document all public endpoints including authentication APIs (/api/admin/login, /api/school/login, /api/student/login), CRUD operations for schools (/api/schools), student management (/api/school/{id}/students), teacher management (/api/school/{id}/teachers), academic year management (/api/academic-years), and subject management (/api/school/{id}/subjects). For each endpoint, specify HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas, authentication requirements, and parameter specifications. Include practical examples of API requests and responses, error handling patterns, and status codes. Document rate limiting, security considerations, and API versioning approach. Address common use cases for each endpoint group and provide integration guidelines for client applications.

wiki_catalogs.name: Two-Factor Authentication
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop documentation for the two-factor authentication (2FA) system implementation. Document PyOTP integration for time-based one-time passwords (TOTP). Explain the 2FA setup process and QR code generation for authenticator apps. Detail the 2FA verification workflow during login and account access. Include practical examples of enabling 2FA for different user roles. Document the backup codes and recovery mechanisms. Address 2FA security considerations and user experience optimization. Provide examples of integrating 2FA with the existing authentication flow. Document error handling for invalid codes and authentication failures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Academic Year Management API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for academic year management endpoints. Document endpoints for managing academic year cycles, including current academic year tracking, year progression logic, and academic year-specific data handling. Include functions for generating upcoming academic years, setting current academic year, updating academic year configurations, and academic year validation rules. Document the academic year database schema, year progression algorithms, and integration with student enrollment and grade tracking systems. Provide examples of academic year setup, year transitions, and data segregation by academic year periods.

wiki_catalogs.name: Reporting and Analytics System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the reporting and analytics system. Explain the performance analytics dashboard including student achievement tracking, class performance metrics, and academic progress monitoring. Document the report generation system for academic transcripts, progress reports, and performance summaries. Detail the data visualization capabilities including grade distribution charts, attendance analytics, and performance trends. Cover the administrative reporting features for school management, teacher evaluation, and academic program assessment. Explain the real-time monitoring system for system performance, user activity tracking, and educational metrics collection. Include examples of report generation workflows, dashboard usage scenarios, and performance monitoring procedures. Address the integration with all educational systems and data aggregation mechanisms.

wiki_catalogs.name: Frontend Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed frontend implementation documentation for the EduFlow Arabic RTL interface system. Describe the role-specific portal architecture including admin dashboard, school dashboard, teacher portal, student portal, and main login page. Document the Arabic language interface implementation with right-to-left layout support, responsive design patterns, and accessibility features. Explain the form validation system, JavaScript components for interactive elements, and CSS design system implementation. Include the unified design system with color schemes, typography, and component styling. Document the relationship between frontend HTML pages and backend API endpoints, including data binding and user interaction patterns. Address cross-browser compatibility, mobile responsiveness, and performance optimization techniques used in the frontend implementation.

wiki_catalogs.name: System Health & Monitoring API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for system health and monitoring endpoints. Document the /health endpoint which provides comprehensive system status including database connectivity, environment detection (Render, Railway, Vercel, Local), configuration validation, and platform-specific warnings. Include health check response schema with status indicators, timestamp information, platform detection, configuration verification, and warning messages for production misconfigurations. Document the health monitoring integration with performance tracking, error reporting, and system diagnostics. Provide examples of health check responses in different deployment environments and troubleshooting guidance based on health status.

wiki_catalogs.name: Educational Features
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the educational management features of the EduFlow system. Detail the student management system including enrollment processes, academic record tracking, and progress monitoring. Document the academic year management system with year progression logic, grade level tracking, and promotion mechanisms. Explain the grade entry and assessment system, attendance monitoring capabilities, and performance analytics features. Include the subject management system with course offerings, teacher assignments, and curriculum tracking. Document reporting systems, data visualization capabilities, and administrative functions. Address the multi-school architecture supporting centralized management across educational institutions. Provide practical examples of educational workflows and system usage patterns for administrators, teachers, and students.

wiki_catalogs.name: Deployment Guide
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive deployment documentation for the EduFlow system. Document hosting platform configurations for Vercel, Render, and Railway including environment variable setup, build processes, and deployment automation. Explain production environment requirements, security configurations, and performance optimization settings. Detail the multi-platform deployment approach supporting cloud providers and traditional hosting environments. Include database deployment strategies, SSL certificate configuration, and monitoring setup. Document environment variable management, secrets handling, and configuration best practices. Address scaling considerations, load balancing, and disaster recovery planning. Provide step-by-step deployment checklists and troubleshooting guides for common deployment issues across different hosting platforms.

wiki_catalogs.name: Development Tools
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create documentation for the EduFlow development tools and AI-assisted development system. Document the .qoder AI agent system with agents, plans, and skills for automated development assistance. Explain the testing framework including test_server.py functionality, service checking utilities, and quality assurance processes. Detail the debugging tools, development workflow optimizations, and code verification systems. Include the AI-powered development assistance capabilities for code generation, refactoring, and feature implementation. Document the relationship between development tools and the overall system architecture. Address how developers can leverage these tools for rapid feature development, testing automation, and maintenance workflows. Provide examples of using AI agents for common development tasks and integration with the existing codebase.

wiki_catalogs.name: Troubleshooting & Maintenance
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive troubleshooting and maintenance documentation for the EduFlow system. Document common issues and their solutions including database connectivity problems, authentication failures, API endpoint errors, and performance bottlenecks. Explain the logging system implementation with edufloiw_logging.py, error tracking mechanisms, and diagnostic tools. Detail maintenance procedures including database cleanup, cache management, log rotation, and system health checks. Include debugging strategies using check_services.py and debug_subjects.py utilities. Document performance monitoring with performance.py, identifying slow queries, memory usage patterns, and optimization opportunities. Address system recovery procedures, backup strategies, and disaster recovery planning. Provide step-by-step troubleshooting guides for common operational issues and preventive maintenance schedules.

wiki_catalogs.name: Contributing & Extensions
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create documentation for contributing to and extending the EduFlow system. Document code contribution guidelines, development standards, and pull request processes. Explain extension points in the modular service architecture that allow adding new features without disrupting existing functionality. Detail customization options for educational institutions including branding modifications, feature additions, and workflow adaptations. Document the enhancement implementation process with examples from BUTTON_FORMATTING_ENHANCEMENT_SUMMARY.md and other improvement documentation. Address backward compatibility considerations, testing requirements for extensions, and integration patterns with existing components. Include guidelines for adding new educational features, modifying the database schema safely, and extending the multi-school architecture. Provide examples of successful feature implementations and best practices for maintaining system stability during extensions.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# EduFlow - Integrated School Management System

## Project Overview

EduFlow is a comprehensive Arabic-language school management system built with modern web technologies. This educational portal provides integrated solutions for managing schools, students, teachers, grades, and attendance across multiple educational stages in Arabic-speaking regions.

### Purpose Statement

EduFlow addresses the complex needs of educational institutions by providing a unified platform that streamlines administrative processes, enhances teaching efficiency, and improves student performance tracking. The system supports the complete educational ecosystem from administration to classroom management.

### Core Goals and Objectives

- **Unified Management**: Centralized control over all school operations including student enrollment, teacher assignments, and academic tracking
- **Arabic Language Support**: Native RTL (Right-to-Left) Arabic interface with culturally appropriate educational content
- **Multi-Role Access Control**: Secure access for administrators, school staff, teachers, and students with role-based permissions
- **Real-time Data Management**: Dynamic updates for grades, attendance, and academic progress
- **Scalable Architecture**: Support for multiple schools and educational stages with centralized academic year management

### Target Audience

- **Administrators**: System-wide oversight and configuration
- **School Administrators**: Daily school operations and student management
- **Teachers**: Classroom management and student performance tracking
- **Students**: Academic progress monitoring and personal information access
- **Parents**: Student progress reporting and communication tools

## Technical Architecture

The system follows a modern microservice-oriented architecture with clear separation of concerns:

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Frontend Client] --> B[API Gateway]
    B --> C[Authentication Service]
    B --> D[School Management Service]
    B --> E[Student Management Service]
    B --> F[Teacher Management Service]
    B --> G[Academic Service]
    
    C --> H[JWT Token Manager]
    D --> I[Database Layer]
    E --> I
    F --> I
    G --> I
    
    I --> J[MySQL Database]
    I --> K[Redis Cache]
    
    L[Security Middleware] --> B
    M[Performance Monitor] --> B
    N[Cache Manager] --> B
```

### Component Breakdown

**Frontend Layer**
- Responsive Arabic interface with RTL support
- Role-based dashboards with dynamic content
- Real-time data synchronization
- Mobile-first design approach

**Backend Services**
- RESTful API with comprehensive CRUD operations
- Modular service architecture for maintainability
- Real-time data processing capabilities
- Multi-stage educational content management

**Data Layer**
- Centralized MySQL database with JSON support
- Redis caching for performance optimization
- Automatic backup and recovery mechanisms
- Scalable storage architecture

### Design Patterns

The system implements several key design patterns:

- **Repository Pattern**: Abstracted database operations for better testability
- **Factory Pattern**: Dynamic service creation based on role requirements
- **Observer Pattern**: Real-time updates for synchronized data states
- **Strategy Pattern**: Pluggable authentication and authorization modules

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

**Server Application** (`server.py`)
- Flask-based REST API with comprehensive routing
- Multi-role authentication system with JWT tokens
- Real-time performance monitoring and logging
- Comprehensive error handling and validation

**Database Management** (`database.py`)
- Dual database support (MySQL and SQLite fallback)
- Advanced schema management with automatic migrations
- Complex relationship handling for multi-school environments
- Performance-optimized query patterns

### Core Modules

**Authentication & Security** (`auth.py`, `security.py`)
- Enhanced JWT token management with refresh capabilities
- Comprehensive input sanitization and validation
- Rate limiting and abuse prevention systems
- Audit logging for compliance and security

**Caching System** (`cache.py`)
- Redis-based distributed caching with in-memory fallback
- Intelligent cache invalidation strategies
- Performance monitoring and optimization
- Scalable cache patterns for different data types

**Performance Monitoring** (`performance.py`)
- Real-time system health monitoring
- Request/response time tracking
- Resource utilization analysis
- Automated performance alerts

### Configuration Approach

**Environment Configuration** (`.env`, `.env.example`)
- Comprehensive environment variable management
- Hosting platform detection and configuration
- Security key management and rotation
- Development vs production environment separation

**Database Schema** (`database.py`)
- Centralized academic year management system
- Flexible grade level and subject assignment
- Multi-school support with isolation boundaries
- Extensible schema for future feature additions

### External Dependencies

**Core Dependencies** (`requirements.txt`)
- Flask 3.0.0 for web framework foundation
- PyJWT 2.8.0 for secure token management
- bcrypt 4.0.1 for password hashing
- mysql-connector-python 8.0.33 for database connectivity
- redis 5.0.1 for caching infrastructure

**Development Tools**
- Python-dotenv for environment management
- bleach 6.1.0 for input sanitization
- pyotp 2.9.0 for two-factor authentication
- qrcode 7.4.2 for QR code generation

### Integration Points

**Frontend Integration** (`public/`)
- Modular JavaScript architecture with reusable components
- CSS-in-JS styling with design system integration
- Responsive design with mobile optimization
- Accessibility features for inclusive education

**API Integration**
- RESTful endpoints with comprehensive documentation
- Real-time WebSocket connections for live updates
- Batch processing capabilities for bulk operations
- Cross-origin resource sharing (CORS) configuration

### Component Relationships

```mermaid
graph LR
    A[User Interface] --> B[API Layer]
    B --> C[Business Logic]
    C --> D[Data Access Layer]
    D --> E[(MySQL Database)]
    D --> F[(Redis Cache)]
    
    G[Security Layer] --> B
    H[Performance Monitor] --> B
    I[Cache Manager] --> D
    
    J[Authentication Service] --> C
    K[Authorization Service] --> C
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**Multi-Stage Educational Management**
- Support for elementary (ابتدائي), intermediate (متوسطة), secondary (ثانوية), and preparatory (إعدادية) levels
- Arabic curriculum alignment with cultural educational standards
- Flexible grade level configurations per school
- Multi-grade classroom management capabilities

**Comprehensive Student Management**
- Detailed student profiles with medical information
- Academic performance tracking across subjects
- Attendance monitoring with historical data
- Personalized learning progress reports

**Advanced Teacher Management**
- Subject specialization tracking
- Classroom assignment management
- Teaching load balancing across schools
- Professional development integration

**Academic Year Management**
- Centralized academic year tracking system
- Multi-year academic progression
- Grade-level advancement automation
- Transcript generation and management

### Implementation Highlights

**Arabic Language Support** (`server.py`)
- Native Arabic interface with RTL layout
- Cultural educational content adaptation
- Arabic numeral and date formatting
- Right-to-left navigation and layouts

**Multi-School Architecture** (`database.py`)
- Isolated data management per educational institution
- Shared administrative capabilities across schools
- Centralized policy enforcement
- Scalable tenant management system

**Real-Time Data Synchronization**
- Live grade updates and notifications
- Instant attendance recording
- Real-time performance metrics
- Synchronized multi-device access

### Feature Architecture

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Authentication
    Authentication --> Dashboard: Valid Credentials
    Authentication --> Error: Invalid Credentials
    
    Dashboard --> SchoolManagement: Admin Role
    Dashboard --> StudentPortal: Student Role
    Dashboard --> TeacherPortal: Teacher Role
    Dashboard --> SchoolDashboard: School Admin Role
    
    SchoolManagement --> StudentManagement
    SchoolManagement --> TeacherManagement
    SchoolManagement --> AcademicManagement
    
    StudentManagement --> GradesTracking
    StudentManagement --> AttendanceMonitoring
    StudentManagement --> ProgressReports
    
    TeacherManagement --> ClassManagement
    TeacherManagement --> GradeEntry
    TeacherManagement --> StudentAssessment
    
    AcademicManagement --> YearSetup
    AcademicManagement --> SubjectAllocation
    AcademicManagement --> ReportGeneration
```

The EduFlow system represents a comprehensive solution for modern educational institutions, combining robust technical architecture with deep cultural and linguistic understanding to serve Arabic-speaking educational communities effectively.

**Sources:**
- [server.py](server.py)
- [database.py](database.py)
- [requirements.txt](requirements.txt)
- [index.html](public/index.html)
- [auth.py](auth.py)
- [security.py](security.py)
- [cache.py](cache.py)
- [.env.example](.env.example)
- [delete_academic_years.sql](delete_academic_years.sql)

</blog>
